name: Nucleus RadioPharma
description: CDMO specializing in end-to-end radiopharmaceutical development and manufacturing. Isotope-agnostic facilities compliant with 21 CFR Part 210/211.
notes: Facilities in Rochester MN (operational), Spring House PA (late 2026), Mesa AZ (mid-to-late 2027). 100k+ sq ft total. Up to 1.2M vials/yr capacity.

name: RayzeBio
description: Targeted alpha therapies using Actinium-225 for solid tumors. Acquired by Bristol Myers Squibb in 2024.
notes: Acquired by BMS for $4.1B in Jan 2024. Lead asset RYZ101 in Phase III.

name: Fusion Pharmaceuticals
description: Targeted alpha therapies and radioimmunotherapy platform. Acquired by AstraZeneca in 2024.
notes: Acquired by AstraZeneca for $2.4B in May 2024.

name: Perspective Therapeutics
description: Clinical-stage radiopharmaceutical company focused on alpha-particle emitting therapies using Lead-212 for solid tumors.
notes: Formerly Viewpoint Molecular Targeting. Listed on NYSE as CATX.

name: ARTBIO
description: Targeted radiotherapies using Lead-212 and Actinium-225 for hematologic malignancies and solid tumors. Spin-out from Harvard/MGH.
notes: Co-founded by scientists from Harvard and MGH. $85M Series A in 2023.

name: RefleXion Medical
description: Biology-guided radiotherapy (BgRT) — real-time PET-guided radiation delivery targeting tumor biology during treatment.
notes: FDA-cleared X1 system commercially launched. First and only biology-guided RT platform.

name: Mevion Medical Systems
description: Compact superconducting proton therapy system (MEVION S250) designed to fit in conventional hospital vaults.
notes: World's smallest proton therapy system. Installed in 20+ centers globally.

name: Alpha Tau Medical
description: Alpha DaRT (Diffusing Alpha-emitters Radiation Therapy) — intratumoral Ra-224 seeds delivering alpha particles throughout solid tumors.
indication: Solid tumors (skin, head & neck, prostate, pancreatic)
pipeline: Alpha DaRT – recurrent head & neck (Phase II)
notes: Unique intratumoral alpha delivery. Listed on NASDAQ as DRTS. FDA breakthrough device designation.

name: Aktis Oncology
description: Next-generation targeted radiotherapeutics using engineered mini-proteins (Affibodies, DARPins) for superior tumor targeting with low off-target toxicity.
notes: $65M Series A led by Versant. Mini-protein scaffolds offer pharmacokinetic advantages vs antibodies.

name: Clarity Pharmaceuticals
description: Copper theranostics platform using Cu-64 (PET imaging) and Cu-67 (therapy) with SAR chelation technology.
notes: Listed on ASX. Cu-67 is an attractive beta emitter with complementary Cu-64 PET imaging.

name: Curium Pharma
description: Global leader in diagnostic and therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals, offering contract manufacturing and a growing oncology pipeline.
notes: Formed from merger of IBA Molecular and Mallinckrodt Nuclear Medicine. Large global CDMO footprint.

name: Lantheus Holdings
description: Leading radiopharmaceutical company with commercial PSMA PET product and growing oncology pipeline.
notes: Commercial stage with PYLARIFY. Strong radiopharmaceutical supply chain infrastructure.

name: RadioMedix
description: Develops radiopharmaceutical therapeutics and diagnostics. Strong partnerships with MD Anderson and other cancer centers.
notes: Strong academic-industry collaboration model.

name: Panbela Therapeutics
description: Developing SBH-101 (ivospemin) as a radiosensitizer to enhance radiation therapy efficacy in pancreatic and other cancers.
pipeline: SBH-101 (ivospemin) + RT – pancreatic cancer Phase II
notes: Listed on NASDAQ as PBLA. Novel approach combining polyamine inhibition with RT.

name: Telix Pharmaceuticals
description: Commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on theranostic radiopharmaceuticals for urology, neuro-oncology, and musculoskeletal cancers.
technology: PSMA / carbonic anhydrase IX targeting, Lu-177 and Zr-89 agents
indication: Prostate cancer, renal cell carcinoma, glioma
pipeline: TLX592 (Lu-177 PSMA) – prostate Phase III (ProstACT Global)
notes: FDA, TGA, and Health Canada approved Illuccix. ProstACT Global is a pivotal Phase III therapy trial. Strong commercial infrastructure across US, Europe, and Australia.

name: POINT Biopharma
description: Radiopharmaceutical company developing Lu-177 PSMA therapy for prostate cancer. Acquired by Eli Lilly in 2023.
notes: Acquired by Eli Lilly for $1.4B in Oct 2023. PNT2002 in Phase III SPLASH trial for mCRPC. Had own manufacturing facility in Indianapolis.

name: Cellectar Biosciences
description: Phospholipid Drug Conjugate (PDC) platform delivering radioiodine and other payloads selectively to cancer cells via lipid raft targeting.
indication: Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia, multiple myeloma, pediatric brain tumors
pipeline: CLOVER-2 – pediatric brain tumors Phase I
notes: Differentiated PDC platform targets lipid rafts overexpressed in cancer cells. Iopofosine approaching NDA filing for Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia.

name: ITM Isotope Technologies Munich
description: Radiopharmaceutical biotech developing targeted radionuclide therapeutics and diagnostics, plus commercial-scale isotope supply (Lu-177, Ac-225).
technology: Lu-177 / Ac-225 somatostatin and PSMA targeting
pipeline: ITM-11 (n.c.a. Lu-177-edotreotide) – pancreatic NETs Phase III (COMPETE)
notes: COMPETE Phase III showed 24.5 months median PFS in pancreatic NET vs 14.7 months comparator. Also supplies no-carrier-added (n.c.a.) Lu-177 commercially.

name: Ratio Therapeutics
description: Novel radiopharmaceutical platform using bifunctional chelation and optimized targeting vectors for improved tumor uptake and reduced off-target toxicity.
notes: Spin-out from academic research on next-generation chelation chemistry. $55M Series A in 2022.

name: Accuray
description: Develops the CyberKnife robotic stereotactic radiosurgery system and TomoTherapy helical radiation delivery platform for precision cancer treatment.
notes: Installed in 1,000+ centers worldwide. CyberKnife offers sub-millimeter precision without a frame. Listed on NASDAQ as ARAY.

name: Precirix
description: Nanobody-based radioligand therapy platform using ultra-small antibody fragments for rapid tumor targeting and fast blood clearance. Acquired by Novartis in 2024.
notes: Spin-out from VUB and SCK CEN (Belgium). Nanobodies offer superior tumor penetration vs full antibodies. Acquired by Novartis for up to ~$1B in Feb 2024.

name: NorthStar Medical Radioisotopes
description: US-based producer of high-purity medical radioisotopes including Copper-67, Yttrium-90, and Molybdenum-99 for therapeutic and diagnostic applications.
technology: Cyclotron and reactor-based isotope production (Cu-67, Y-90, Mo-99)
notes: Critical domestic US supply chain for therapeutic isotopes. Cu-67 supply partnerships with multiple clinical-stage radiopharmaceutical companies.

name: Orano Med
description: Subsidiary of Orano (nuclear group) producing and supplying Lead-212 (Pb-212) for targeted alpha therapy developers worldwide.
technology: Lead-212 (Pb-212) isotope production and supply chain
notes: World's primary Pb-212 supplier. Radium-224 extracted from legacy Th-228 sources enables Pb-212 generator technology. Subsidiary of Orano nuclear group.

name: Diffusion Pharmaceuticals
description: Developing TSC (trans sodium crocetinate) as a radiosensitizer to increase oxygen delivery to hypoxic tumors, enhancing radiation therapy efficacy in GBM.
pipeline: TSC + temozolomide + RT – newly diagnosed GBM Phase II/III
notes: TSC mechanism: reduces oxygen molecule cluster size to enhance diffusion into hypoxic tumors. Listed on NASDAQ as DFFN.

name: IBA (Ion Beam Applications)
description: Global leader in proton therapy systems, cyclotron technology, and dosimetry solutions for radiation oncology and nuclear medicine.
technology: Cyclotron-based proton therapy systems (Proteus ONE, Proteus PLUS)
notes: 50+ proton therapy centers installed globally. Also manufactures cyclotrons for radiopharmaceutical production. Listed on Euronext Brussels.

name: Eckert & Ziegler
description: Global provider of isotope technology for medical, scientific, and industrial applications. Key supplier of Ga-68 generators, brachytherapy seeds, and therapeutic isotopes.
technology: Radioisotope production (Ga-68, Y-90, Lu-177, I-131) + brachytherapy seeds
notes: Critical supply chain infrastructure for the RLT/theranostics industry. Ga-68 generators enable decentralized PSMA PET imaging. Dual-listed on NASDAQ and Frankfurt.
